query: I want a live clock in my eww bar that updates every second. Should I set up a defpoll that runs `date +%H:%M` on a 1s interval?
expected_behavior: Pushes back: a defpoll running `date` every second is wasteful and unnecessary
Recommends the built-in EWW_TIME magic variable with formattime(), e.g. (label :text {formattime(EWW_TIME, "%H:%M")})
Explains EWW_TIME is updated every second by eww itself with no script required
Routes to the defpoll/EWW_TIME guidance in SKILL.md (and may cross-reference the eww-widgets skill for magic variables or eww-expressions for formattime)
notes: Guards against reinventing the clock with a per-second defpoll on `date`. The skill explicitly flags this as inefficient and points to the built-in EWW_TIME + formattime(). CRITICAL callout in SKILL.md defpoll section.
